page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture instance includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the integrated 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,420.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 93,157.6, both placing in the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show uncached read speeds of 287.5 GB/sec and write speeds of 120.71 GB/sec. For database operations, the server demonstrated top-tier performance with 32,548.32 operations in Passmark database tests and 109.38 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

The hardware profile offers high resource density, combining massive local storage capacity with high-speed DDR5 memory. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-throughput local data access without the latency of network-attached storage. The presence of Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) supports accelerated mathematical and cryptography operations, though the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy parallel deep learning training.

This server is designed for data-intensive workloads, including large-scale relational and non-relational databases, high-throughput static web serving, and memory-intensive data processing. It also supports medium-parameter language model prompt processing, achieving 292.73 tokens per second on 7B models.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8559C cores running at 3.6 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 50 Gbps baseline network speed. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier capabilities, scoring 93,157.6 in Passmark CPU tests and achieving 32,548.32 in Passmark database operations. Its memory subsystem delivers uncached read speeds of 287.5 GB/sec. The hardware configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density storage and memory-heavy tasks, offering built-in AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets. This instance is optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput static web serving, and data-intensive processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory platform featuring sixty terabytes of local NVMe SSD and ninety-six Intel Xeon vCPUs.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8559C cores running at 3.6 GHz
60,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
768.0 GB of DDR5 memory with 287.5 GB/sec uncached read bandwidth
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations and multi-threaded decompression
Best-fit for large-scale databases, static web serving, and data-intensive processing
